# Game project structure and organization (2026-03-06 19:37Z)

_**User**_

I need to understand how the existing games in this project are structured. This is a monorepo with a Next.js web app at apps/web/ and a server at apps/server/.

Please explore:
1. The overall project structure (top-level files, packages, apps)
2. How existing games are organized — look at apps/web/app/games/ to see all game routes, and apps/web/components/games/ to see all game components
3. Pick one game (e.g. "the-line" or "guessio") and understand its full structure: the route pages (page.tsx for lobby, host/page.tsx, player/page.tsx) and the corresponding components (Host, Player)
4. How the room/lobby system works — look at apps/web/components/room/ 
5. How GameLayout works — apps/web/components/layout/GameLayout.tsx
6. What shared infrastructure exists (websocket, state management, etc.)

Be very thorough. Return a detailed summary of:
- Project architecture (monorepo structure, key packages)
- Game routing pattern (what routes each game has)
- Game component pattern (what components each game has, props they receive)
- Room/lobby flow (how players join, how host starts)
- Real-time communication mechanism (websocket, server events, etc.)
- Any shared types or game-logic packages
- The UI framework and styling approach (Tailwind? shadcn? custom?)

---

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Tracing host flows**

I identified two host flow patterns: direct room creation for The Line/Demo, and lobby-based flow for Guessio via /host endpoint. I traced the host flow for Guessio: /host?game=guessio  RoomHost creates room  shows QR + player list  host clicks start  socket sends start event  server sets status to playing. I found that RoomHost redirects host to game page instead of rendering game UI because children() returns null. I'm tracing the player flow from join to game start and usePartyRoom with sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby' reads the storage key incorrectly. I'm tracing the player flow from join to game start and discovered that Guessio requires sessionId in URL but Join redirect doesn't pass it. I found that Guessio requires sessionId in URL but Join redirect doesn't pass it, causing a redirect to /join when session is missing. </details></think>
